rows.locality: Site 1040 near Wadi Halfa dromedary context
rows.geocoder_or_gazetteer: named-place resolution to Wadi Halfa city anchor
rows.confidence_rationale: The archived article names Site 1040 near Wadi Halfa explicitly. The current coordinate is an approximate city-level geocode for Wadi Halfa rather than a published excavation coordinate pair.

chronology.original_text: Historical and archaeological dromedary range context

inclusion_notes: Camel now keeps one named-place resolution trail instead of only a broad Arabian/Levant centroid.
rows.paper_title: Combined hybridization capture and shotgun sequencing for ancient DNA analysis of extinct wild and domestic dromedary camel
rows.exact_source_text: The remains of a single large-sized Late Pleistocene camel individual recovered from the Site 1040 near Wadi Halfa were first evaluated by Gautier.
rows.nordic_inclusion_reason: The current curated project inventory does not yet identify a shipped Nordic-localized lead for this record.
rows.interpretation_note: Camel now keeps one named-place resolution trail instead of only a broad Arabian/Levant centroid.

rows.confidence_rationale: The archived paper names Botai explicitly, but does not ship a direct coordinate pair in the local source archive, so the current coordinate is an approximate named-site geocode.
rows.original_place_text: Botai culture of the Central Asian steppes
inclusion_notes: Botai remains the only clearly named domesticated-core horse site that currently earns point-level publication.
rows.paper_title: Ancient genomes revisit the ancestry of domestic and Przewalski's horses
rows.exact_source_text: The Eneolithic Botai culture of the Central Asian steppes provides the earliest archaeological evidence for horse husbandry, ~5500 years ago, but the exact nature of early horse domestication remains controversial.
rows.nordic_inclusion_reason: The current curated project inventory does not yet identify a shipped Nordic-localized lead for this record.
rows.interpretation_note: Botai remains the only clearly named domesticated-core horse site that currently earns point-level publication.
